title: ADR-0074 F8: operator worklist surface for console-routed items — `evolve inbox` console section + a stable terminal triage bucket (not per-cycle 'deferred' churn)
summary: Architect review of ADR-0074 (finding 8, LOW): console-routed items stay physically in .evolve/inbox (correct — single backlog view, no claim races) but the operator's own command hides the distinction: cmd_inbox.go classifies ALL items without PartitionConsole, and triage's completeness check re-buckets excluded items as 'deferred' every cycle — unbounded churn + prompt budget. Fix: (1) `evolve inbox` (and `evolve inbox batches`) renders a distinct 'console-routed (operator-owned)' section via inboxbatch.PartitionConsole(items, guards.IsProtectedSurface) with per-item reasons; (2) triage contract gains a stable `console_routed[]` terminal bucket so excluded items are accounted once, not re-deferred per cycle.
acceptance: RED: evolve inbox output separates console-routed items with reasons (fixture with route field + protected-files derivation)
RED: triage completeness accounting accepts console_routed[] as a terminal bucket (no per-cycle re-deferral)
go test -race on touched pkgs PASS; apicover clean
notes: [SCOPE 2026-07-23] Triage-side console partition landed; remaining scope is ONLY the cmd_inbox.go PartitionConsole rendering.
